Unit 1: Problem Solving Process

In this unit, I learned how to approach problems logically and structure solutions using input, processing, and output (IPO). My project involved creating flowcharts to solve different problems—such as calculating areas, converting units, and computing averages. I focused on clarity, organization, and making sure each step followed a logical flow. This project helped me understand the foundation of programming and how clear structure leads to fewer errors later on.

I also learned how to think algorithmically—breaking down a complex task into smaller steps. Below is a link to my completed flowchart file and a small code-style example that shows the same logic in pseudocode.

View My Flowchart PDF

Example Code Snippet


INPUT: side length (s)
PROCESS: area = s * s
OUTPUT: area
